description: The GTM instance is the core resource in Cloud Scheduling GTM for hosting scheduling domain names, routing policies, and health check configurations. After creating a GTM instance, you can use it to configure global traffic scheduling, failover, and health check capabilities for your business domain name

AccessMode.description: Access method for the GTM instance. `cname` means a scheduling domain name is generated and you configure CNAME at your DNS provider; `domain` means Cloud DNS directly resolves the business domain name to the target address

AlarmId.description: Alarm rule ID associated with the GTM instance

Cname.description: Scheduling domain name of the GTM instance

CreateTime.description: Creation time of the GTM instance. Accurate to milliseconds, formatted according to ISO 8601 standard

Domain.description: Domain name of the GTM instance, such as `www.example.com`

LastOperator.description: Account that last operated the GTM instance

Owner.description: Account that created the GTM instance

PolicyType.description: Routing policy type. `geo` indicates basic routing policy, `perf` indicates intelligent routing policy

ProjectName.description: Project name to which the GTM instance belongs

Remark.description: Remark information for the GTM instance

SpecName.description: Feature version of the GTM instance. `ultimate` indicates flagship edition, `standard` indicates standard edition

State.description: Current lifecycle status of the GTM instance, such as `init`: indicates the GTM instance has been successfully created but not yet configured. `configuring_address`: indicates basic rule configuration is complete but target address configuration is not yet finished. `editing`: indicates both basic configuration and target address are completed but the instance is not yet enabled. `running`: indicates the instance is officially enabled

Ttl.description: Expiration time of the DNS record, in seconds

UpdateTime.description: Most recent modification time of the GTM instance. Accurate to milliseconds, formatted according to ISO 8601 standard

ZoneType.description: This parameter has no practical significance and can be ignored
